body_txt: Barefooters are often in tune with their surroundings and deeply understand people, animals and the outdoors in general. These characters usually live on the outskirts of civilization in a rural, coastal or even tribal Close-Knit Community . They can sense and react to the terrain “connected” in a way that shoes or socks would hinder . With sharper senses and reflexes, it might result in quirks such as ticklishness, or other Reflexive Responses . They might also have thick, supple soles covered in dirt, but Beauty Is Never Tarnished typically rules this out. Additionally, they can usually understand animal behavior , and probably have at least one Action Pet . In general, they are a Nature Lover who is always barefoot outdoors. They are typically free spirited, innocent, energetic, idealistic, and often some form of conservative, or just stubborn in general, but they are primarily humble, (“down to earth” if you like). Content without status, wealth or worldly grandeur. Their vulnerability to the terrain and openness to their surroundings reflect deep empathy for all life , especially those of their community, making them to especially heroic. They might even embrace Barefoot Poverty out of a heart for those less fortunate than them. This aspect might make this an Invoked for holy characters or when they are in the awesome presence of God himself, like Moses at the burning bush. Because of their rural tradition based lifestyle, and their tendency to be “grounded” in the moment, they tend to focus on friends, family and culture rather than science and utilitarianism, so they are generally highly grounded in tradition or conservative. This can range from a highly religious Magical Native American of Africa to a Fundamentalist from they Deep South, Sure, they might fight the Evil Poacher to protect their Loyal Animal Companion , or stand up to the Evil Colonialist to defend their home, but it’s a concern for their immediate friends and communities rather than saving the world or fixing society. This also means that this trope is surprisingly sometimes at odds with Green Aesop , especially if you have a rural barefooter who is skeptical of climate change..They also tend to enjoy hunting and fishing, participating as part the ecosystem rather than taking an off hands approach, which can cause this trope to clash with Hunting Is Evil . Of course the Granola Girl is often the exception here, but she typically comes off as superstitious rather than genuine. On the other hand, Boots of Toughness reflect the more pragmatic aspect of environmentalist characters and ecologists ; responsible, but not free spirited. Even Tarzan donned boots when he switched to a more direct Green Aesop . Despite their tendency towards dogma or tribalism, there is also positive side to their conservatism, often manifesting as a very principled form of Lawful Good , strongly rooted in their culture and humility. These might include values of honesty, patriotism (or pride for their tribe), a good work ethic, treating animals humanely, or family values. So while might not want to bring justice to society, they might also be very pure of heart, focused on individual good. Whether their traditional, culturally grounded worldview is portrayed as backwards and old fashioned or honorable, will very from author to author. This trope is often used alongside the shod Corrupt Corporate Executive , Evil Colonialist , Granola Girl , or The Fundamentalist , to contrast them to the down to earth barefooter who understands nature personally. This trope is often paired with holy and humble characters, like the Virgin Mary. The Hot Gypsy Woman , Farmer's Daughter , Jungle Princess and Noble Savage are frequent examples if not unofficial sub tropes, so adding shoes can cause dissonance. Due to Mother Nature, Father Science , most of these characters are female. Related to Fertile Feet . Subtrope of Prefers Going Barefoot (in most cases). Compare with Barefoot Poverty , Barefoot Sage . They rarely have superpowers unless they are a Magical Barefooter . Contrast Barefoot Loon , where they the can be barefoot as a sign of detachment from the present moment rather than being connected to their surroundings. Truth in Television in most moderate to warm rural areas, especially for those from Australia, New Zealand and the American South.
